summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the 'Weapons' series with Part 6, focusing on 'The Weapon of Our Words.' He opened by reminding the congregation that they are in a daily spiritual battle, but as children of God, no weapon formed against them will prosper. The central message was that our words are a powerful spiritual tool given by God, capable of bringing either death or life. Pastor Travis established that our words reveal what is in our hearts and are shaped by what we consume. He then presented five practical ways to wield this weapon for God's purposes: by being thoughtful, truthful, kind, encouraging, and positive. The sermon was grounded in wisdom from Proverbs and the Book of James, emphasizing that controlling our speech is a sign of spiritual maturity and a primary way we 'shine like stars' in a dark world. He concluded with a call to put on our new nature in Christ, leaving behind old patterns of speech.
mainPoints: Our words are a powerful spiritual weapon that reveal the condition of our hearts and our spiritual intake.
We must intentionally use words that are thoughtful and truthful, thinking before we speak and rejecting gossip and slander.
As followers of Christ, we are called to use words that are kind, encouraging, and positive to build others up and shine God's light.

versesMentioned.text: The weapons we fight with are not the weapons of the world. On the contrary, they have divine power to demolish strongholds.
Be alert and of sober mind. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.

No weapon forged against you will prevail, and you will refute every tongue that accuses you. This is the heritage of the servants of the LORD, and this is their vindication from me, declares the LORD.
The tongue has the power of life and death, and those who love it will eat its fruit.

With the tongue we praise our Lord and Father, and with it we curse human beings, who have been made in God’s likeness. Out of the same mouth come praise and cursing. My brothers and sisters, this should not be.
A good man brings good things out of the good stored up in his heart, and an evil man brings evil things out of the evil stored up in his heart. For the mouth speaks what the heart is full of.
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.
May these words of my mouth and this meditation of my heart be pleasing in your sight, LORD, my Rock and my Redeemer.
My dear brothers and sisters, take note of this: Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.
The words of the reckless pierce like swords, but the tongue of the wise brings healing.
Set a guard over my mouth, LORD; keep watch over the door of my lips.
Those who guard their mouths and their tongues keep themselves from calamity.
Truthful lips endure forever, but a lying tongue lasts only a moment.
The words of a gossip are like choice morsels; they go down to the inmost parts.
Without wood a fire goes out; without a gossip a quarrel dies down.
so that you may become blameless and pure, “children of God without fault in a warped and crooked generation.” Then you will shine among them like stars in the sky.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness and self-control. Against such things there is no law.
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
The soothing tongue is a tree of life, but a perverse tongue crushes the spirit.
Therefore encourage one another and build each other up, just as in fact you are doing.

But now you must also rid yourselves of all such things as these: anger, rage, malice, slander, and filthy language from your lips. Do not lie to each other, since you have taken off your old self with its practices and have put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge in the image of its Creator.

Monday.scripture: Proverbs 18:21 (NIV) - The tongue has the power of life and death, and those who love it will eat its fruit.
Monday.action: Pay close attention to everything you say today. Mentally note if your words are tending toward life or death.
Monday.prayer: Lord, set a guard over my mouth today. Help me to be aware of the power of my words.

Tuesday.scripture: James 1:19 (NIV) - Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.
Tuesday.action: In every conversation today, consciously pause for three seconds before you respond. Listen fully first.
Tuesday.prayer: God, slow me down. Make me quick to listen and slow to speak, reflecting Your patience.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 12:19 (NIV) - Truthful lips endure forever, but a lying tongue lasts only a moment.
Wednesday.action: Commit to absolute honesty in all your communication today. Avoid even 'small' exaggerations or gossip.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, root my heart in truth. Let my words be honest and build trust, refusing gossip or slander.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 15:1 (NIV) - A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
Thursday.action: If someone is rude or difficult with you today, intentionally respond with a gentle and kind word.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, produce Your fruit of kindness in me. Help me to offer a gentle answer, especially when it's hard.

Friday.scripture: 1 Thessalonians 5:11 (NIV) - Therefore encourage one another and build each other up, just as in fact you are doing.
Friday.action: Intentionally speak 10 words of encouragement to different people today—family, friends, coworkers, or even a stranger.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, make me a conduit of Your encouragement. Show me who needs a word that instills courage today.

Saturday.scripture: Colossians 3:8, 10 (NIV) - But now you must also rid yourselves of all such things as these: anger, rage, malice, slander, and filthy language... and have put on the new self...
Saturday.action: Reflect on your speech this week. Identify one old pattern (e.g., complaining, negativity) and consciously replace it with a positive, God-honoring alternative.
Saturday.prayer: Creator God, I choose to put on my new nature in Christ. Renew my mind and my mouth to be more like You.
